title: <name> and the Secret of the Disappearing Bells

pages.text: The sun was just rising over the little town of Nandipur. The air smelled of jasmine and incense, and everyone was busy preparing for the temple fair. <name> helped Amma decorate rangolis near the gate, while Papa hung bright marigold garlands. But that morning, something strange happened. When the temple priest came to ring the golden bells — ding-ding! — he frowned. “Two bells are missing!” he said.

<name> gasped. “Missing? But who would take temple bells?” The next day, more bells vanished. The villagers were puzzled. So <name> decided to solve the mystery herself. She tied her hair into two braids, grabbed her notebook, and whispered, “Detective <name> is on the case!”

That evening, she looked carefully around the temple steps. Tiny paw prints dotted the ground near the bell stand. “Hmm,” she said, “too small for a dog… maybe a monkey?” She followed the prints past a sleepy cow, who blinked lazily. “Moo… I didn’t see anyone,” it seemed to say.

Near the banyan tree, <name> noticed flower petals scattered everywhere — and a faint jingling sound from above. She looked up. “Aha!” There, in the high branches, a cheeky monkey was busy tying the golden bells to a string of flowers! He shook them proudly — jingle-jingle!

<name> laughed. “So you’re the bell thief! But why?” The monkey chattered happily and pointed to the big tree — it was covered in shiny bells and bright petals, like a golden Diwali tree! When <name> told the priest, he smiled. “Our monkey friend wanted to decorate for the fair too!”

So, instead of taking the bells down, the villagers added colorful ribbons and lights. That night, the whole tree sparkled under the moon, filling the air with ding-ding-jingle-jingle! music. <name> smiled proudly. “Mystery solved — and the prettiest surprise ever!”
